coarse_alignment_reason: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.
chinese_text:
東方未明，顛倒衣裳。顛之倒之，自公召之。
東方未晞，顛倒裳衣。倒之顛之，自公令之。
折柳樊圃，狂夫瞿瞿。不能辰夜，不夙則莫。
translation_text:
1 Before the east was bright,
I was putting on my clothes upside down ;
I was putting them on upside down,
And there was one from the court calling me.

2 Before there was a streak of claw-n in the east,
I was putting on my clothes upside down;
I was putting them on upside down,
And there was one from the court with orders for me.

3 You fence your garden with branches of willow,
And the reckless fellows stand in awe.
He, [however], cannot fix the time of night;
If he be not too early, he is sure to be late.
